# ChainKit

## What is ChainKit?
Multi-chain wallet SDK. 30 blockchains, unified API, zero external chain SDK dependencies.
All crypto via Cure53-audited @noble/@scure libraries. Pure TypeScript.

## Installation
npm install @chainkit/client @chainkit/ethereum @chainkit/bitcoin @chainkit/solana
# Install only the chains you need. Full list:
# @chainkit/{ethereum,bitcoin,solana,tron,ton,cosmos,aptos,sui,near,cardano,
#   xrp,stellar,starknet,stacks,kaia,eos,polkadot,hedera,filecoin,icp,
#   algorand,vechain,tezos,theta,multiversx,iota,neo,flow,icon,mina}

## Quick Example

```typescript
import { createClient } from '@chainkit/client'
import { ethereum } from '@chainkit/ethereum'

const client = await createClient({
  chains: {
    ethereum: {
      chain: ethereum,
      rpcs: ['https://eth-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY'],
      privateKey: '0x...',
    },
  },
})

// Read
const balance = await client.ethereum.getBalance('0x...')

// Write (auto nonce + fee + sign + broadcast)
const txHash = await client.ethereum.send({ to: '0x...', amount: '1000000000000000000' })

// Wait for confirmation
const confirmed = await client.ethereum.waitForTransaction(txHash)

// Clean up key material
client.ethereum.destroy()
```

## API Reference

### ChainSigner
Every chain signer implements these methods:
- generateMnemonic(strength?: number): string -- 128=12 words, 256=24 words
- validateMnemonic(mnemonic: string): boolean
- derivePrivateKey(mnemonic: string, hdPath: string): Promise<string> | string
- getAddress(privateKey: string): string
- validateAddress(address: string): boolean
- signTransaction(params: { privateKey: string, tx: UnsignedTx }): Promise<string>
- signMessage(params: { privateKey: string, message: string | Uint8Array }): Promise<string> | string
- getDefaultHdPath(): string

Usage:
```typescript
import { EthereumSigner } from '@chainkit/ethereum'
const signer = new EthereumSigner()
const mnemonic = signer.generateMnemonic()
const pk = await signer.derivePrivateKey(mnemonic, signer.getDefaultHdPath())
const address = signer.getAddress(pk)
const signed = await signer.signTransaction({
  privateKey: pk,
  tx: { to: '0x...', value: '1000', fee: { gasLimit: '0x5208' }, extra: { chainId: 1 } },
})
```

### ChainProvider
Every chain provider implements:
- getBalance(address: string): Promise<{ address, amount, symbol, decimals }>
- getNonce(address: string): Promise<string | number>
- getTransaction(hash: string): Promise<TransactionInfo | null>
- getBlock(hashOrNumber: string | number): Promise<BlockInfo | null>
- estimateFee(): Promise<{ slow, average, fast, unit }>
- broadcastTransaction(signedTx: string): Promise<string>
- getChainInfo(): Promise<{ chainId, name, symbol, decimals, testnet, blockHeight }>
- waitForTransaction(hash: string, options?: { timeoutMs?, intervalMs? }): Promise<TransactionInfo>

Usage:
```typescript
import { EthereumProvider } from '@chainkit/ethereum'
const provider = new EthereumProvider({
  endpoints: ['https://eth-mainnet.example.com'],
  strategy: 'failover',
  timeout: 10000,
  retries: 2,
})
const balance = await provider.getBalance('0x...')
```

### Capabilities (optional chain extensions)
- ContractCapable: callContract(address, method, params), estimateGas(address, method, params)
- TokenCapable: getTokenBalance(address, tokenAddress), getTokenMetadata(tokenAddress), getMultipleTokenBalances(address, tokenAddresses[])
- UtxoCapable: getUtxos(address), selectUtxos(address, targetAmount)
- SubscriptionCapable: subscribeBlocks(callback), subscribeTransactions(address, callback)
- EvmSignerCapable: signTypedData({ privateKey, domain, types, primaryType, message }) -- EIP-712, available on Ethereum and Kaia signers

### Unified Client
```typescript
import { createClient } from '@chainkit/client'
import { ethereum } from '@chainkit/ethereum'

const client = await createClient({
  chains: {
    ethereum: {
      chain: ethereum,
      rpcs: ['https://...'],
      privateKey: '0x...',        // omit for read-only
      network: 'mainnet',         // 'mainnet' | 'testnet'
      strategy: 'failover',       // 'failover' | 'round-robin' | 'fastest'
      mnemonic: '...',            // alternative to privateKey
      hdPath: "m/44'/60'/0'/0/0", // optional, uses chain default
    },
  },
})

// Read methods (all chains)
client.ethereum.getBalance(address)
client.ethereum.getTransaction(hash)
client.ethereum.getBlock(numberOrHash)
client.ethereum.estimateFee()
client.ethereum.getChainInfo()
client.ethereum.waitForTransaction(hash, { timeoutMs: 60000, intervalMs: 3000 })

// Write methods (when privateKey or mnemonic provided)
client.ethereum.send({ to, amount, memo?, data?, options? })  // auto nonce+fee, sign, broadcast
client.ethereum.prepareTransaction({ to, amount })             // build unsigned tx only
client.ethereum.signTransaction({ privateKey, tx })            // low-level sign
client.ethereum.signMessage({ privateKey, message })           // sign message
client.ethereum.getAddress()                                   // derived address

// Lifecycle
client.ethereum.destroy()     // zero out stored key material
client.ethereum.provider      // access underlying ChainProvider
client.ethereum.signer        // access underlying ChainSigner
```

Security: signing clients using 'fastest' strategy are automatically downgraded to 'failover'.

### ABI Encoder
```typescript
import { encodeFunctionCall, decodeFunctionResult, ERC20 } from '@chainkit/core'

// Encode function call
const data = encodeFunctionCall('transfer(address,uint256)', ['0x...', 1000000n])

// ERC-20 presets
encodeFunctionCall(ERC20.transfer, ['0x...', 1000000n])
encodeFunctionCall(ERC20.approve, ['0x...', 1000000n])
encodeFunctionCall(ERC20.balanceOf, ['0x...'])
// Also: ERC20.allowance, ERC20.totalSupply, ERC20.decimals, ERC20.symbol, ERC20.name

// Decode return data
const [balance] = decodeFunctionResult(['uint256'], returnHex)

// Supported types: address, uint*, int*, bool, bytes32, string, bytes
// Limitation: dynamic arrays (uint256[]) not supported
```

### SecureKey
```typescript
import { SecureKey } from '@chainkit/core'
const key = new SecureKey('0xabc...')  // or Uint8Array
key.hex      // '0xabc...'
key.bytes    // Uint8Array
key.destroy() // zeros key material, .hex/.bytes throw after this
key.isDestroyed // true
```

### Default HD Paths
| Chain | HD Path |
|-------|---------|
| Ethereum | m/44'/60'/0'/0/0 |
| Bitcoin (mainnet) | m/84'/0'/0'/0/0 |
| Bitcoin (testnet) | m/84'/1'/0'/0/0 |
| Solana | m/44'/501'/0'/0' |
| Tron | m/44'/195'/0'/0/0 |
| TON | m/44'/607'/0'/0'/0' |
| Cosmos | m/44'/118'/0'/0/0 |
| Aptos | m/44'/637'/0'/0'/0' |
| Sui | m/44'/784'/0'/0'/0' |
| NEAR | m/44'/397'/0' |
| Cardano | m/1852'/1815'/0'/0/0 |
| XRP | m/44'/144'/0'/0/0 |
| Stellar | m/44'/148'/0' |
| StarkNet | m/44'/9004'/0'/0/0 |
| Stacks | m/44'/5757'/0'/0/0 |
| Kaia | m/44'/8217'/0'/0/0 |
| EOS | m/44'/194'/0'/0/0 |
| Polkadot | m/44'/354'/0'/0/0 |
| Hedera | m/44'/3030'/0'/0/0 |
| Filecoin | m/44'/461'/0'/0/0 |
| ICP | m/44'/223'/0'/0/0 |
| Algorand | m/44'/283'/0'/0/0 |
| VeChain | m/44'/818'/0'/0/0 |
| Tezos | m/44'/1729'/0'/0' |
| Theta | m/44'/500'/0'/0/0 |
| MultiversX | m/44'/508'/0'/0'/0' |
| IOTA | m/44'/4218'/0'/0'/0' |
| Neo | m/44'/888'/0'/0/0 |
| Flow | m/44'/539'/0'/0/0 |
| Icon | m/44'/4801074'/0'/0/0 |
| Mina | m/44'/12586'/0'/0/0 |

### Signature Algorithms
| Category | Curve | Chains |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Secp256k1 | secp256k1 ECDSA | Ethereum, Bitcoin, Tron, Cosmos, XRP, Stacks, Kaia, EOS, Filecoin, VeChain, Theta, Icon |
| ED25519 | Ed25519 EdDSA | Solana, TON, Aptos, Sui, NEAR, Cardano, Stellar, Hedera, ICP, Algorand, Tezos, MultiversX, IOTA |
| SR25519 | Schnorrkel/Ristretto | Polkadot |
| STARK | Stark curve | StarkNet |
| Secp256r1 | NIST P-256 ECDSA | Neo |
| ECDSA_P256 | ECDSA P-256 | Flow |
| Pasta | Pallas curve Schnorr | Mina |

## Security
- All crypto via Cure53-audited @noble/@scure libraries
- Private key zeroing via SecureKey (Uint8Array, explicit destroy())
- Address validation before signing
- ChainId required for EVM transactions (no cross-chain replay)
- strictHttps option for RPC endpoints
- Nonce mutex for concurrent sends
- RPC response ID validation
- URL redaction in error messages (prevents API key leakage)
- Bitcoin fee sanity check (max 50% of input value)
- Network enforcement (Bitcoin mainnet/testnet address validation)
- Strategy auto-downgrade (fastest -> failover for signing clients)
- Input sanitization (strips outputs/inputs from send options)
- Full audit report: docs/security-audit-report-2026-04.md

## Limitations
- 4/30 chains not testnet-verified for balance/tx (Neo, Flow, Icon, IOTA) -- address derivation verified for all 30
- No mainnet verification yet
- ABI encoder does not support dynamic arrays (uint256[])
- Hedera requires HederaEcdsaSigner for EVM relay mode (default HederaSigner uses ED25519)
- Subscriptions are poll-based (HTTP), not WebSocket
- EIP-712 signTypedData available on Ethereum and Kaia signers only

## RPC Strategies
- failover: try endpoints in order, fall back on failure
- round-robin: distribute across endpoints
- fastest: race all endpoints, use first response
- All strategies: auto-retry on network errors (configurable retries, default 2), per-request timeout (default 10000ms)
- strictHttps: true rejects non-HTTPS endpoints (except localhost)

## Error Handling
All errors are ChainKitError with a code property:
- NETWORK_ERROR, TIMEOUT, INVALID_PARAMS, INVALID_ADDRESS, INVALID_MNEMONIC
- INVALID_PATH, INVALID_PRIVATE_KEY, TRANSACTION_FAILED, INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE
- RPC_ALL_FAILED, RPC_ERROR, RPC_INVALID_RESPONSE
- UNSUPPORTED_CHAIN, UNSUPPORTED_FEATURE, SIGNING_FAILED, UNKNOWN
